content:
Let \(a<391\) and write \(h=\gcd(a,391)\in\{1,17,23\}\) and \(g=\gcd(a,b)\).  Then
\[
\lcm(a,b)>\lcm(a,391)
\iff \frac b g>\frac{391}{h}.
\]
– If \(h=1\), we need \(b>391g\), so the smallest \(b\) is \(392\).  
– If \(h=17\), we need \(b>23g\), so the smallest \(b\) is \(24\).  
– If \(h=23\), we need \(b>17g\), so the smallest \(b\) is \(18\).  

In fact, taking \(a=23\) (so \(\gcd(a,391)=23\) and \(\gcd(a,18)=1\)) gives
\[
\lcm(23,18)=414>\lcm(23,391)=391,
\]
and one checks no smaller \(b\) can work.  Hence the minimum possible \(b\) is

\(\boxed{18}\).

message.content:
Let \(a<391\) and write \(h=\gcd(a,391)\in\{1,17,23\}\) and \(g=\gcd(a,b)\).  Then
\[
\lcm(a,b)>\lcm(a,391)
\iff \frac b g>\frac{391}{h}.
\]
– If \(h=1\), we need \(b>391g\), so the smallest \(b\) is \(392\).  
– If \(h=17\), we need \(b>23g\), so the smallest \(b\) is \(24\).  
– If \(h=23\), we need \(b>17g\), so the smallest \(b\) is \(18\).  

In fact, taking \(a=23\) (so \(\gcd(a,391)=23\) and \(\gcd(a,18)=1\)) gives
\[
\lcm(23,18)=414>\lcm(23,391)=391,
\]
and one checks no smaller \(b\) can work.  Hence the minimum possible \(b\) is

\(\boxed{18}\).
